name: textblob

metadata.name: textblob
metadata.summary: Simple, Pythonic text processing. Sentiment analysis, part-of-speech tagging, noun phrase parsing, and more.

documentation:
# TextBlob: Simplified Text Processing

```{image} https://badgen.net/pypi/v/TextBlob
:alt: Latest version
:target: https://pypi.org/project/textblob/
```

```{image} https://github.com/sloria/TextBlob/actions/workflows/build-release.yml/badge.svg
:alt: Build status
:target: https://github.com/sloria/TextBlob/actions/workflows/build-release.yml
```

Homepage: [https://textblob.readthedocs.io/](https://textblob.readthedocs.io/)

`TextBlob` is a Python library for processing textual data. It provides a simple API for diving into common natural language processing (NLP) tasks such as part-of-speech tagging, noun phrase extraction, sentiment analysis, classification, translation, and more.

```python
from textblob import TextBlob

text = """
The titular threat of The Blob has always struck me as the ultimate movie
monster: an insatiably hungry, amoeba-like mass able to penetrate
virtually any safeguard, capable of--as a doomed doctor chillingly
describes it--"assimilating flesh on contact.
Snide comparisons to gelatin be damned, it's a concept with the most
devastating of potential consequences, not unlike the grey goo scenario
proposed by technological theorists fearful of
artificial intelligence run rampant.
"""

blob = TextBlob(text)
blob.tags  # [('The', 'DT'), ('titular', 'JJ'),
#  ('threat', 'NN'), ('of', 'IN'), ...]

blob.noun_phrases  # WordList(['titular threat', 'blob',
#            'ultimate movie monster',
#            'amoeba-like mass', ...])

for sentence in blob.sentences:
    print(sentence.sentiment.polarity)
# 0.060
# -0.341
```

TextBlob stands on the giant shoulders of [NLTK] and [pattern], and plays nicely with both.

## Features

- Noun phrase extraction
- Part-of-speech tagging
- Sentiment analysis
- Classification (Naive Bayes, Decision Tree)
- Tokenization (splitting text into words and sentences)
- Word and phrase frequencies
- Parsing
- `n`-grams
- Word inflection (pluralization and singularization) and lemmatization
- Spelling correction
- Add new models or languages through extensions
- WordNet integration

## Get it now

```
$ pip install -U textblob
$ python -m textblob.download_corpora
```

## Examples

See more examples at the [Quickstart guide].

## Documentation

Full documentation is available at <https://textblob.readthedocs.io/>.

## Project Links

- Docs: <https://textblob.readthedocs.io/>
- Changelog: <https://textblob.readthedocs.io/en/latest/changelog.html>
- PyPI: <https://pypi.python.org/pypi/TextBlob>
- Issues: <https://github.com/sloria/TextBlob/issues>

## License

MIT licensed. See the bundled [LICENSE](https://github.com/sloria/TextBlob/blob/master/LICENSE) file for more details.

[nltk]: http://nltk.org/
[pattern]: https://github.com/clips/pattern/
[quickstart guide]: https://textblob.readthedocs.io/en/latest/quickstart.html#quickstart

modules.name: textblob.download_corpora
documentation:
Downloads the necessary NLTK corpora for TextBlob.

Usage:

```
$ python -m textblob.download_corpora
```

If you only intend to use TextBlob's default models, you can use the "lite"
option:

```
$ python -m textblob.download_corpora lite
```
!/usr/bin/env python

modules.name: textblob.sentiments
documentation:
Default sentiment analyzers are English for backwards compatibility, so
you can still do

```pycon
>>> from textblob.sentiments import PatternAnalyzer
```

which is equivalent to

```pycon
>>> from textblob.en.sentiments import PatternAnalyzer
```

functions.name: textblob.utils.strip_punc
documentation:
Removes punctuation from a string.

```
param s

The string.

param all

Remove all punctuation. If False, only removes punctuation from
the ends of the string.
```

functions.name: textblob.utils.lowerstrip
documentation:
Makes text all lowercase and strips punctuation and whitespace.

```
param s

The string.

param all

Remove all punctuation. If False, only removes punctuation from
the ends of the string.
```

functions.name: textblob.utils.tree2str
documentation:
Convert a nltk.tree.Tree to a string.

For example:
    (NP a/DT beautiful/JJ new/JJ dashboard/NN) -> "a beautiful dashboard"

functions.name: textblob.utils.filter_insignificant
documentation: Filter out insignificant (word, tag) tuples from a chunk of text.

functions.name: textblob.utils.is_filelike
documentation: Return whether `obj` is a file-like object.

modules.name: textblob.inflect
documentation:
Make word inflection default to English. This allows for backwards
compatibility so you can still import text.inflect.

    >>> from textblob.inflect import singularize

is equivalent to

    >>> from textblob.en.inflect import singularize

modules.name: textblob.parsers
documentation:
Default parsers to English for backwards compatibility so you can still do

```pycon
>>> from textblob.parsers import PatternParser
```

which is equivalent to

```pycon
>>> from textblob.en.parsers import PatternParser
```

classes.name: textblob.unicodecsv.UnicodeWriter
documentation:
```pycon
>>> import unicodecsv
>>> from cStringIO import StringIO
>>> f = StringIO()
>>> w = unicodecsv.writer(f, encoding='utf-8')
>>> w.writerow((u'é', u'ñ'))
>>> f.seek(0)
>>> r = unicodecsv.reader(f, encoding='utf-8')
>>> row = r.next()
>>> row[0] == u'é'
True
>>> row[1] == u'ñ'
True
```

classes.name: textblob.unicodecsv.DictWriter
documentation:
```pycon
>>> from cStringIO import StringIO
>>> f = StringIO()
>>> w = DictWriter(f, ['a', u'ñ', 'b'], restval=u'î')
>>> w.writerow({'a':'1', u'ñ':'2'})
>>> w.writerow({'a':'1', u'ñ':'2', 'b':u'ø'})
>>> w.writerow({'a':u'é', u'ñ':'2'})
>>> f.seek(0)
>>> r = DictReader(f, fieldnames=['a', u'ñ'], restkey='r')
>>> r.next() == {'a': u'1', u'ñ':'2', 'r': [u'î']}
True
>>> r.next() == {'a': u'1', u'ñ':'2', 'r': [u'Ã¸']}
True
>>> r.next() == {'a': u'Ã©', u'ñ':'2', 'r': [u'Ã®']}
True
```

classes.name: textblob.unicodecsv.DictReader
documentation:
```pycon
>>> from cStringIO import StringIO
>>> f = StringIO()
>>> w = DictWriter(f, fieldnames=['name', 'place'])
>>> w.writerow({'name': 'Cary Grant', 'place': 'hollywood'})
>>> w.writerow({'name': 'Nathan Brillstone', 'place': u'øLand'})
>>> w.writerow({'name': u'Willam ø. Unicoder', 'place': u'éSpandland'})
>>> f.seek(0)
>>> r = DictReader(f, fieldnames=['name', 'place'])
>>> print r.next() == {'name': 'Cary Grant', 'place': 'hollywood'}
True
>>> print r.next() == {'name': 'Nathan Brillstone', 'place': u'øLand'}
True
>>> print r.next() == {'name': u'Willam ø. Unicoder', 'place': u'éSpandland'}
True
```

modules.name: textblob.taggers
documentation:
Default taggers to the English taggers for backwards incompatibility, so you
can still do

```pycon
>>> from textblob.taggers import NLTKTagger
```

which is equivalent to

```pycon
>>> from textblob.en.taggers import NLTKTagger
```

modules.name: textblob.formats
documentation:
File formats for training and testing data.

Includes a registry of valid file formats. New file formats can be added to the
registry like so:

```
from textblob import formats

class PipeDelimitedFormat(formats.DelimitedFormat):
    delimiter = '|'

formats.register('psv', PipeDelimitedFormat)
```

Once a format has been registered, classifiers will be able to read data files with
that format.

```
from textblob.classifiers import NaiveBayesAnalyzer

with open('training_data.psv', 'r') as fp:
    cl = NaiveBayesAnalyzer(fp, format='psv')
```

functions.name: textblob.formats.detect
documentation:
Attempt to detect a file's format, trying each of the supported
formats. Return the format class that was detected. If no format is
detected, return `None`.

functions.name: textblob.formats.get_registry
documentation: Return a dictionary of registered formats.

functions.name: textblob.formats.register
documentation:
Register a new format.

:param str name: The name that will be used to refer to the format, e.g. 'csv'
:param type format_class: The format class to register.

documentation: Return an iterable object from the data.

documentation:
Detect the file format given a filename.
Return True if a stream is this file format.

:::{versionchanged} 0.9.0 Changed from a static method to a class method.
:::

Interface for format classes. Individual formats can decide on the
composition and meaning of `**kwargs`.

```

A file-like object.
```

:::{versionchanged} 0.9.0 Constructor receives a file pointer rather than a file path.
:::

documentation: Return True if stream is valid.
A general character-delimited format.

classes.name: textblob.formats.CSV
documentation:
CSV format. Assumes each row is of the form `text,label`.

```
Today is a good day,pos
I hate this car.,pos
```

classes.name: textblob.formats.TSV
documentation: TSV format. Assumes each row is of the form `text      label`.

classes.name: textblob.formats.JSON

methods.name: textblob.formats.JSON.to_iterable
documentation: Return an iterable object from the JSON data.

methods.name: textblob.formats.JSON.detect
documentation: Return True if stream is valid JSON.

JSON format.

Assumes that JSON is formatted as an array of objects with `text` and
`label` properties.

```
[
    {"text": "Today is a good day.", "label": "pos"},
    {"text": "I hate this car.", "label": "neg"}
]
```

variables.name: textblob.exceptions.TextBlobException
documentation: Backwards compat

variables.name: textblob.exceptions.MissingCorpusException
documentation: Backwards compat

classes.name: textblob.exceptions.TextBlobError
documentation: A TextBlob-related error.

classes.name: textblob.exceptions.MissingCorpusError
documentation:
Exception thrown when a user tries to use a feature that requires a
dataset or model that the user does not have on their system.

classes.name: textblob.exceptions.DeprecationError
documentation: Raised when user uses a deprecated feature.

classes.name: textblob.exceptions.TranslatorError
documentation: Raised when an error occurs during language translation or detection.

classes.name: textblob.exceptions.NotTranslated
documentation:
Raised when text is unchanged after translation. This may be due to the language
being unsupported by the translator.

classes.name: textblob.exceptions.FormatError
documentation: Raised if a data file with an unsupported format is passed to a classifier.

modules.name: textblob.classifiers
documentation:
Various classifier implementations. Also includes basic feature extractor
methods.

Example Usage:

```
>>> from textblob import TextBlob
>>> from textblob.classifiers import NaiveBayesClassifier
>>> train = [
...     ('I love this sandwich.', 'pos'),
...     ('This is an amazing place!', 'pos'),
...     ('I feel very good about these beers.', 'pos'),
...     ('I do not like this restaurant', 'neg'),
...     ('I am tired of this stuff.', 'neg'),
...     ("I can't deal with this", 'neg'),
...     ("My boss is horrible.", "neg")
... ]
>>> cl = NaiveBayesClassifier(train)
>>> cl.classify("I feel amazing!")
'pos'
>>> blob = TextBlob("The beer is good. But the hangover is horrible.", classifier=cl)
>>> for s in blob.sentences:
...     print(s)
...     print(s.classify())
...
The beer is good.
pos
But the hangover is horrible.
neg
```

:::{versionadded} 0.6.0
:::

functions.name: textblob.classifiers._get_words_from_dataset
documentation:
Return a set of all words in a dataset.

```
param dataset

A list of tuples of the form (words, label) where
words is either a string of a list of tokens.
```
## Basic feature extractors ###

functions.name: textblob.classifiers.basic_extractor
documentation:
A basic document feature extractor that returns a dict indicating
what words in `train_set` are contained in `document`.

```
param document

The text to extract features from. Can be a string or an iterable.

param list train_set

Training data set, a list of tuples of the form
(words, label) OR an iterable of strings.
```

functions.name: textblob.classifiers.contains_extractor
documentation:
A basic document feature extractor that returns a dict of words that
the document contains.

documentation:
Reads a data file and returns an iterable that can be used
as testing or training data.

documentation: The classifier object.

methods.name: textblob.classifiers.BaseClassifier.classify
documentation: Classifies a string of text.

methods.name: textblob.classifiers.BaseClassifier.train
documentation: Trains the classifier.

methods.name: textblob.classifiers.BaseClassifier.labels
documentation: Returns an iterable containing the possible labels.

methods.name: textblob.classifiers.BaseClassifier.extract_features
documentation:
Extracts features from a body of text.

```
rtype

dictionary of features
```

Abstract classifier class from which all classifers inherit. At a
minimum, descendant classes must implement a `classify` method and have
a `classifier` property.

```
param train_set

The training set, either a list of tuples of the form
(text, classification) or a file-like object. text may be either
a string or an iterable.

param callable feature_extractor

A feature extractor function that takes one or
two arguments: document and train_set.

param str format

If train_set is a filename, the file format, e.g.
"csv" or "json". If None, will attempt to detect the
file format.

param kwargs

Additional keyword arguments are passed to the constructor
of the  class used to
read the data. Only applies when a file-like object is passed as
train_set.
```

:::{versionadded} 0.6.0
:::
#### CLASSIFIERS #####

classes.name: textblob.classifiers.NLTKClassifier

methods.name: textblob.classifiers.NLTKClassifier.classifier
documentation: The classifier.

methods.name: textblob.classifiers.NLTKClassifier.train
documentation:
Train the classifier with a labeled feature set and return
the classifier. Takes the same arguments as the wrapped NLTK class.
This method is implicitly called when calling `classify` or
`accuracy` methods and is included only to allow passing in arguments
to the `train` method of the wrapped NLTK class.

:::{versionadded} 0.6.2
:::

```
rtype

A classifier
```

documentation: Return an iterable of possible labels.

documentation:
Classifies the text.

:param str text: A string of text.

methods.name: textblob.classifiers.NLTKClassifier.accuracy
documentation:
Compute the accuracy on a test set.

```
param test_set

A list of tuples of the form (text, label), or a
file pointer.

param format

If test_set is a filename, the file format, e.g.
"csv" or "json". If None, will attempt to detect the
file format.
```

methods.name: textblob.classifiers.NLTKClassifier.update
documentation:
Update the classifier with new training data and re-trains the
classifier.

```
param new_data

New data as a list of tuples of the form
(text, label).
```

An abstract class that wraps around the nltk.classify module.

Expects that descendant classes include a class variable `nltk_class`
which is the class in the nltk.classify module to be wrapped.

Example:

```
class MyClassifier(NLTKClassifier):
    nltk_class = nltk.classify.svm.SvmClassifier
```

classes.name: textblob.classifiers.NaiveBayesClassifier

methods.name: textblob.classifiers.NaiveBayesClassifier.prob_classify
documentation:
Return the label probability distribution for classifying a string
of text.

Example:

```
>>> classifier = NaiveBayesClassifier(train_data)
>>> prob_dist = classifier.prob_classify("I feel happy this morning.")
>>> prob_dist.max()
'positive'
>>> prob_dist.prob("positive")
0.7
```

```
rtype

nltk.probability.DictionaryProbDist
```

```

```

Displays a listing of the most informative features for this

```

A classifier based on the Naive Bayes algorithm, as implemented in
NLTK.

```

The training set, either a list of tuples of the form
(text, classification) or a filename. text may be either
a string or an iterable.

param feature_extractor

A feature extractor function that takes one or
two arguments: document and train_set.

param format

If train_set is a filename, the file format, e.g.
"csv" or "json". If None, will attempt to detect the

```

A classifier based on the decision tree algorithm, as implemented in

```
param train_set

The training set, either a list of tuples of the form
(text, classification) or a filename. text may be either

param feature_extractor

A feature extractor function that takes one or

param format

If train_set is a filename, the file format, e.g.
"csv" or "json". If None, will attempt to detect the

classes.name: textblob.classifiers.PositiveNaiveBayesClassifier

methods.name: textblob.classifiers.PositiveNaiveBayesClassifier.train
documentation:
Train the classifier with a labeled and unlabeled feature sets and return
the classifier. Takes the same arguments as the wrapped NLTK class.
This method is implicitly called when calling `classify` or
`accuracy` methods and is included only to allow passing in arguments
to the `train` method of the wrapped NLTK class.

```
rtype

A classifier
```
Override

methods.name: textblob.classifiers.PositiveNaiveBayesClassifier.update
documentation:
Update the classifier with new data and re-trains the
classifier.

```
param new_positive_data

List of new, labeled strings.

param new_unlabeled_data

List of new, unlabeled strings.
```

A variant of the Naive Bayes Classifier that performs binary
classification with partially-labeled training sets, i.e. when only
one class is labeled and the other is not. Assuming a prior distribution
on the two labels, uses the unlabeled set to estimate the frequencies of
the features.

Example usage:

```
>>> from text.classifiers import PositiveNaiveBayesClassifier
>>> sports_sentences = ['The team dominated the game',
...                   'They lost the ball',
...                   'The game was intense',
...                   'The goalkeeper catched the ball',
...                   'The other team controlled the ball']
>>> various_sentences = ['The President did not comment',
...                        'I lost the keys',
...                        'The team won the game',
...                        'Sara has two kids',
...                        'The ball went off the court',
...                        'They had the ball for the whole game',
...                        'The show is over']
>>> classifier = PositiveNaiveBayesClassifier(positive_set=sports_sentences,
...                                           unlabeled_set=various_sentences)
>>> classifier.classify("My team lost the game")
True
>>> classifier.classify("And now for something completely different.")
False
```

```
param positive_set

A collection of strings that have the positive label.

param unlabeled_set

A collection of unlabeled strings.

param feature_extractor

A feature extractor function.

param positive_prob_prior

A prior estimate of the probability of the
label True.
```

:::{versionadded} 0.7.0
:::

methods.name: textblob.classifiers.MaxEntClassifier.prob_classify
documentation:
Return the label probability distribution for classifying a string
of text.

Example:

```
>>> classifier = MaxEntClassifier(train_data)
>>> prob_dist = classifier.prob_classify("I feel happy this morning.")
>>> prob_dist.max()
'positive'
>>> prob_dist.prob("positive")
0.7
```

```
rtype

nltk.probability.DictionaryProbDist
```

modules.name: textblob.wordnet
documentation:
Wordnet interface. Contains classes for creating Synsets and Lemmas
directly.

:::{versionadded} 0.7.0
:::

modules.name: textblob.tokenizers
documentation:
Various tokenizer implementations.

:::{versionadded} 0.4.0
:::

functions.name: textblob.tokenizers.word_tokenize
documentation:
Convenience function for tokenizing text into words.

NOTE: NLTK's word tokenizer expects sentences as input, so the text will be
tokenized to sentences before being tokenized to words.

variables.name: textblob.tokenizers._word_tokenizer
documentation: Singleton word tokenizer

classes.name: textblob.tokenizers.WordTokenizer

methods.name: textblob.tokenizers.WordTokenizer.tokenize
documentation:
Return a list of word tokens.

```
param text

string of text.

param include_punc

(optional) whether to
include punctuation as separate tokens. Default to True.
```

NLTK's recommended word tokenizer (currently the TreeBankTokenizer).
Uses regular expressions to tokenize text. Assumes text has already been
segmented into sentences.

Performs the following steps:

* split standard contractions, e.g. don't -> do n't
* split commas and single quotes
* separate periods that appear at the end of line

classes.name: textblob.tokenizers.SentenceTokenizer

methods.name: textblob.tokenizers.SentenceTokenizer.tokenize
documentation: Return a list of sentences.

NLTK's sentence tokenizer (currently PunktSentenceTokenizer).
Uses an unsupervised algorithm to build a model for abbreviation words,
collocations, and words that start sentences,
then uses that to find sentence boundaries.

modules.name: textblob.np_extractors
documentation:
Default noun phrase extractors are for English to maintain backwards
compatibility, so you can still do

```pycon
>>> from textblob.np_extractors import ConllExtractor
```

which is equivalent to

```pycon
>>> from textblob.en.np_extractors import ConllExtractor
```

classes.name: textblob.mixins.ComparableMixin
documentation: Implements rich operators for an object.

classes.name: textblob.mixins.BlobComparableMixin
documentation: Allow blob objects to be comparable with both strings and blobs.

classes.name: textblob.mixins.StringlikeMixin

methods.name: textblob.mixins.StringlikeMixin.__repr__
documentation: Returns a string representation for debugging.

methods.name: textblob.mixins.StringlikeMixin.__str__
documentation:
Returns a string representation used in print statements
or str(my_blob).

methods.name: textblob.mixins.StringlikeMixin.__len__
documentation: Returns the length of the raw text.

methods.name: textblob.mixins.StringlikeMixin.__iter__
documentation:
Makes the object iterable as if it were a string,
iterating through the raw string's characters.

methods.name: textblob.mixins.StringlikeMixin.__contains__
documentation: Implements the `in` keyword like a Python string.

methods.name: textblob.mixins.StringlikeMixin.__getitem__
documentation:
Returns a  substring. If index is an integer, returns a Python
string of a single character. If a range is given, e.g. `blob[3:5]`,
a new instance of the class is returned.

methods.name: textblob.mixins.StringlikeMixin.find
documentation:
Behaves like the built-in str.find() method. Returns an integer,
the index of the first occurrence of the substring argument sub in the
sub-string given by [start:end].

methods.name: textblob.mixins.StringlikeMixin.rfind
documentation:
Behaves like the built-in str.rfind() method. Returns an integer,
the index of he last (right-most) occurence of the substring argument
sub in the sub-sequence given by [start:end].

methods.name: textblob.mixins.StringlikeMixin.index
documentation:
Like blob.find() but raise ValueError when the substring
is not found.

methods.name: textblob.mixins.StringlikeMixin.rindex
documentation:
Like blob.rfind() but raise ValueError when substring is not
found.

methods.name: textblob.mixins.StringlikeMixin.startswith
documentation: Returns True if the blob starts with the given prefix.

methods.name: textblob.mixins.StringlikeMixin.endswith
documentation: Returns True if the blob ends with the given suffix.

methods.name: textblob.mixins.StringlikeMixin.title
documentation: Returns a blob object with the text in title-case.

methods.name: textblob.mixins.StringlikeMixin.format
documentation:
Perform a string formatting operation, like the built-in
`str.format(*args, **kwargs)`. Returns a blob object.

methods.name: textblob.mixins.StringlikeMixin.split
documentation: Behaves like the built-in str.split().

methods.name: textblob.mixins.StringlikeMixin.strip
documentation:
Behaves like the built-in str.strip([chars]) method. Returns
an object with leading and trailing whitespace removed.

methods.name: textblob.mixins.StringlikeMixin.upper
documentation: Like str.upper(), returns new object with all upper-cased characters.

methods.name: textblob.mixins.StringlikeMixin.lower
documentation: Like str.lower(), returns new object with all lower-cased characters.

methods.name: textblob.mixins.StringlikeMixin.join
documentation:
Behaves like the built-in `str.join(iterable)` method, except
returns a blob object.

Returns a blob which is the concatenation of the strings or blobs
in the iterable.

methods.name: textblob.mixins.StringlikeMixin.replace
documentation:
Return a new blob object with all the occurence of `old` replaced
by `new`.

Make blob objects behave like Python strings.

Expects that classes that use this mixin to have a _strkey() method that
returns the string to apply string methods to. Using _strkey() instead
of __str__ ensures consistent behavior between Python 2 and 3.

modules.name: textblob.blob
documentation:
Wrappers for various units of text, including the main
{class}`TextBlob <textblob.blob.TextBlob>`, {class}`Word <textblob.blob.Word>`,
and {class}`WordList <textblob.blob.WordList>` classes.
Example usage:

```
>>> from textblob import TextBlob
>>> b = TextBlob("Simple is better than complex.")
>>> b.tags
[(u'Simple', u'NN'), (u'is', u'VBZ'), (u'better', u'JJR'), (u'than', u'IN'), (u'complex', u'NN')]
>>> b.noun_phrases
WordList([u'simple'])
>>> b.words
WordList([u'Simple', u'is', u'better', u'than', u'complex'])
>>> b.sentiment
(0.06666666666666667, 0.41904761904761906)
>>> b.words[0].synsets()[0]
Synset('simple.n.01')
```

:::{versionchanged} 0.8.0 These classes are now imported from ``textblob`` rather than ``text.blob``.
:::

methods.name: textblob.blob.Blobber.__call__
documentation:

`pos_tagger`, `tokenizer`, `analyzer`, and `classifier`.

```
returns

A new .
```

A factory for TextBlobs that all share the same tagger,
tokenizer, parser, classifier, and np_extractor.

Usage:

> ```pycon
> >>> from textblob import Blobber
> >>> from textblob.taggers import NLTKTagger
> >>> from textblob.tokenizers import SentenceTokenizer
> >>> tb = Blobber(pos_tagger=NLTKTagger(), tokenizer=SentenceTokenizer())
> >>> blob1 = tb("This is one blob.")
> >>> blob2 = tb("This blob has the same tagger and tokenizer.")
> >>> blob1.pos_tagger is blob2.pos_tagger
> True
> ```

```
param tokenizer

(optional) A tokenizer instance. If None,
defaults to .

param np_extractor

(optional) An NPExtractor instance. If None,
defaults to .

param pos_tagger

(optional) A Tagger instance. If None,
defaults to .

param analyzer

(optional) A sentiment analyzer. If None,
defaults to .

param parser

A parser. If None, defaults to
.

param classifier

A classifier.
```

:::{versionadded} 0.4.0
:::

functions.name: textblob.en.inflect.pluralize
documentation:
Returns the plural of a given word.
For example: child -> children.
Handles nouns and adjectives, using classical inflection by default
(e.g. where "matrix" pluralizes to "matrices" instead of "matrixes").
The custom dictionary is for user-defined replacements.

modules.name: textblob._text
documentation:
This file is adapted from the pattern library.

URL: http://www.clips.ua.ac.be/pages/pattern-web
Licence: BSD

functions.name: textblob._text.decode_string
documentation: Returns the given value as a Unicode string (if possible).
String functions

functions.name: textblob._text.encode_string
documentation: Returns the given value as a Python byte string (if possible).

functions.name: textblob._text.penntreebank2universal
documentation: Returns a (token, tag)-tuple with a simplified universal part-of-speech tag.

functions.name: textblob._text.find_tokens
documentation:
Returns a list of sentences. Each sentence is a space-separated string of tokens (words).
Handles common cases of abbreviations (e.g., etc., ...).
Punctuation marks are split from other words. Periods (or ?!) mark the end of a sentence.
Headings without an ending period are inferred by line breaks.

functions.name: textblob._text._read
documentation:
Returns an iterator over the lines in the file at the given path,
stripping comments and decoding each line to Unicode.
Pattern's text parsers are based on Brill's algorithm.
Brill's algorithm automatically acquires a lexicon of known words,
and a set of rules for tagging unknown words from a training corpus.
Lexical rules are used to tag unknown words, based on the word morphology (prefix, suffix, ...).
Contextual rules are used to tag all words, based on the word's role in the sentence.
Named entity rules are used to discover proper nouns (NNP's).

functions.name: textblob._text._suffix_rules
documentation: Default morphological tagging rules for English, based on word suffixes.

functions.name: textblob._text.find_tags
documentation:
Returns a list of [token, tag]-items for the given list of tokens:
["The", "cat", "purs"] => [["The", "DT"], ["cat", "NN"], ["purs", "VB"]]
Words are tagged using the given lexicon of (word, tag)-items.
Unknown words are tagged NN by default.
Unknown words that start with a capital letter are tagged NNP (unless language="de").
Unknown words that consist only of digits and punctuation marks are tagged CD.
Unknown words are then improved with morphological rules.
All words are improved with contextual rules.
If a model is given, uses model for unknown words instead of morphology and context.
If map is a function, it is applied to each (token, tag) after applying all rules.

functions.name: textblob._text.find_chunks
documentation:
The input is a list of [token, tag]-items.
The output is a list of [token, tag, chunk]-items:
The/DT nice/JJ fish/NN is/VBZ dead/JJ ./. =>
The/DT/B-NP nice/JJ/I-NP fish/NN/I-NP is/VBZ/B-VP dead/JJ/B-ADJP ././O

functions.name: textblob._text.find_prepositions
documentation:
The input is a list of [token, tag, chunk]-items.
The output is a list of [token, tag, chunk, preposition]-items.
PP-chunks followed by NP-chunks make up a PNP-chunk.

variables.name: textblob._text.RE_ABBR1
documentation: single letter, "T. De Smedt"

variables.name: textblob._text.RE_ABBR2
documentation: alternating letters, "U.S."

classes.name: textblob._text.lazydict

methods.name: textblob._text.lazydict._lazy
documentation:
If the dictionary is empty, calls lazydict.load().
Replaces lazydict.method() with dict.method() and calls it.
A lazy dictionary is empty until one of its methods is called.
This way many instances (e.g., lexicons) can be created without using memory until used.

methods.name: textblob._text.lazylist._lazy
documentation:
If the list is empty, calls lazylist.load().
Replaces lazylist.method() with list.method() and calls it.

documentation:
A dictionary of words and their part-of-speech tags.
For unknown words, rules for word morphology, context and named entities can be used.

classes.name: textblob._text.Rules

methods.name: textblob._text.Rules.apply
documentation: Applies the rule to the given token or list of tokens.
Brill's algorithm generates lexical (i.e., morphological) rules in the following format:
NN s fhassuf 1 NNS x => unknown words ending in -s and tagged NN change to NNS.
ly hassuf 2 RB x => unknown words ending in -ly change to RB.

methods.name: textblob._text.Morphology.__init__
documentation: A list of rules based on word morphology (prefix, suffix).

methods.name: textblob._text.Morphology.apply
documentation: Applies lexical rules to the given token, which is a [word, tag] list.

methods.name: textblob._text.Morphology.insert
documentation:
Inserts a new rule that assigns the given tag to words with the given affix,
e.g., Morphology.append("RB", "-ly").

classes.name: textblob._text.Context

methods.name: textblob._text.Context.__init__
documentation: A list of rules based on context (preceding and following words).

methods.name: textblob._text.Context.apply
documentation:
Applies contextual rules to the given list of tokens,
where each token is a [word, tag] list.

methods.name: textblob._text.Context.insert
documentation:
Inserts a new rule that updates words with tag1 to tag2,
given constraints x and y, e.g., Context.append("TO < NN", "VB")
Brill's algorithm generates contextual rules in the following format:
VBD VB PREVTAG TO => unknown word tagged VBD changes to VB if preceded by a word tagged TO.

methods.name: textblob._text.Entities.__init__
documentation:
A dictionary of named entities and their labels.
For domain names and e-mail adresses, regular expressions are used.

methods.name: textblob._text.Entities.apply
documentation:
Applies the named entity recognizer to the given list of tokens,
where each token is a [word, tag] list.

methods.name: textblob._text.Entities.append
documentation:
Appends a named entity to the lexicon,
e.g., Entities.append("Hooloovoo", "PERS")

methods.name: textblob._text.Score.__new__
documentation: A (polarity, subjectivity)-tuple with an assessments property.

methods.name: textblob._text.Sentiment.__init__
documentation:
A dictionary of words (adjectives) and polarity scores (positive/negative).
The value for each word is a dictionary of part-of-speech tags.
The value for each word POS-tag is a tuple with values for
polarity (-1.0-1.0), subjectivity (0.0-1.0) and intensity (0.5-2.0).

methods.name: textblob._text.Sentiment.load
documentation:
Loads the XML-file (with sentiment annotations) from the given path.
By default, Sentiment.path is lazily loaded.

methods.name: textblob._text.Sentiment.synset
documentation:
Returns a (polarity, subjectivity)-tuple for the given synset id.
For example, the adjective "horrible" has id 193480 in WordNet:
Sentiment.synset(193480, pos="JJ") => (-0.6, 1.0, 1.0).

methods.name: textblob._text.Sentiment.__call__
documentation:
Returns a (polarity, subjectivity)-tuple for the given sentence,
with polarity between -1.0 and 1.0 and subjectivity between 0.0 and 1.0.
The sentence can be a string, Synset, Text, Sentence, Chunk, Word, Document, Vector.
An optional weight parameter can be given,
as a function that takes a list of words and returns a weight.

methods.name: textblob._text.Sentiment.assessments
documentation:
Returns a list of (chunk, polarity, subjectivity, label)-tuples for the given list of words:
where chunk is a list of successive words: a known word optionally
preceded by a modifier ("very good") or a negation ("not good").

methods.name: textblob._text.Sentiment.annotate
documentation:
Annotates the given word with polarity, subjectivity and intensity scores,
and optionally a semantic label (e.g., MOOD for emoticons, IRONY for "(!)").

methods.name: textblob._text.Parser.__init__
documentation:
A simple shallow parser using a Brill-based part-of-speech tagger.
The given lexicon is a dictionary of known words and their part-of-speech tag.
The given default tags are used for unknown words.
Unknown words that start with a capital letter are tagged NNP (except for German).
Unknown words that contain only digits and punctuation are tagged CD.
The given language can be used to discern between
Germanic and Romance languages for phrase chunking.

methods.name: textblob._text.Parser.find_tokens
documentation:
Returns a list of sentences from the given string.
Punctuation marks are separated from each word by a space.

methods.name: textblob._text.Parser.find_tags
documentation:
Annotates the given list of tokens with part-of-speech tags.
Returns a list of tokens, where each token is now a [word, tag]-list.

methods.name: textblob._text.Parser.find_chunks
documentation:
Annotates the given list of tokens with chunk tags.
Several tags can be added, for example chunk + preposition tags.

methods.name: textblob._text.Parser.find_prepositions
documentation: Annotates the given list of tokens with prepositional noun phrase tags.

methods.name: textblob._text.Parser.find_labels
documentation: Annotates the given list of tokens with verb/predicate tags.

methods.name: textblob._text.Parser.find_lemmata
documentation: Annotates the given list of tokens with word lemmata.

methods.name: textblob._text.Parser.parse
documentation:
Takes a string (sentences) and returns a tagged Unicode string (TaggedString).
        Sentences in the output are separated by newlines.
        With tokenize=True, punctuation is split from words and sentences are separated by 
.
        With tags=True, part-of-speech tags are parsed (NN, VB, IN, ...).
        With chunks=True, phrase chunk tags are parsed (NP, VP, PP, PNP, ...).
        With relations=True, semantic role labels are parsed (SBJ, OBJ).
        With lemmata=True, word lemmata are parsed.
        Optional parameters are passed to
        the tokenizer, tagger, chunker, labeler and lemmatizer.

methods.name: textblob._text.TaggedString.__new__
documentation:
Unicode string with tags and language attributes.
For example: TaggedString("cat/NN/NP", tags=["word", "pos", "chunk"]).

methods.name: textblob._text.TaggedString.split
documentation:
Returns a list of sentences, where each sentence is a list of tokens,
where each token is a list of word + tags.

classes.name: textblob._text.Spelling

methods.name: textblob._text.Spelling.train
documentation:
Counts the words in the given string and saves the probabilities at the given path.
This can be used to generate a new model for the Spelling() constructor.

methods.name: textblob._text.Spelling._edit1
documentation: Returns a set of words with edit distance 1 from the given word.

methods.name: textblob._text.Spelling._edit2
documentation: Returns a set of words with edit distance 2 from the given word

methods.name: textblob._text.Spelling._known
documentation: Returns the given list of words filtered by known words.

methods.name: textblob._text.Spelling.suggest
documentation:
Return a list of (word, confidence) spelling corrections for the given word,
based on the probability of known words with edit distance 1-2 from the given word.
Based on: Peter Norvig, "How to Write a Spelling Corrector", http://norvig.com/spell-correct.html
